steps.title: Spot the forward progression
steps.content: The passage opens with “Beginning in her days at Vassar College in 1905” and then moves to describing what happened to her advocacy work. The strongest clue is the progression from that beginning to her eventual national prominence, which requires a transition word showing forward movement in time.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
”Ultimately,” meaning “in the end,” is the correct transition because it shows that her advocacy work eventually led to prominence.

The other choices either indicate immediate consequence, mischaracterize the second part as an example of her causes, or suggest parallel situations.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
